rdf:Description.rdamd:P30137: F 17280cam a2201225 i 4500 F001 99161955228601452 F005 20240813213015.0 F006 ea a f 0 F008 050628m19001901dcuab b f001 0 eng d F035 ## $a (OCoLC)60761971 F035 ## $a (OCoLC)ocm60761971 F035 ## $a (EXLNZ-01ALLIANCE_NETWORK)99322195927401451 F035 ## $a (01ALLIANCE_UW)99161955228601452 F040 ## $a UNL $b eng $e rda $c UNL $d MTG $d OCLCQ $d OCLCF $d OCLCO $d OCLCQ $d WAU $d OCLCA $d OCLCO $d OCLCQ $d OCLCL $d MTG $d OCLCO $d MTG F041 ## $a eng F043 ## $a n-us--- $a n-us-ak $a n-us-tx $a a-ph--- F049 ## $a MAIN F050 14 $a QE75 $b .G46 1900 F086 0# $a I 19.1:900/1-7 F086 0# $a I 19.1:900/5/maps F086 0# $a I 19.1:900/6 continued F245 00 $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 / $c by Charles D. Walcott. F246 33 $a 21st annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900. F246 15 $a Twenty-first annual report of the director of the United States Geological Survey F246 33 $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 $n Part I $p Director's report, including triangulation, primary traverse, and spirit leveling. F246 33 $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 $n Part II $p General geology, economic geology, Alaska. F246 33 $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 $n Part III $p General geology, ore and phosphate deposits Philippines. F246 33 $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 $n Part IV $p Hydrography. F246 33 $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 $n Part V $p Forest Reserves. F246 33 $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 $n Part V (maps) $p Forest Reserves. F246 33 $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 $n Part VI $p Mineral resources of the United States, 1899, metallic products, coal and coke. F246 33 $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 $n Part VI (continued) $p Mineral resources of the United States, 1899, Nonmetallic products, except coal and coke. F246 33 $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 $n Part VII $p Texas. F264 #1 $a Washington D.C. : $b United States Government Printing Office, $c 1900-1901. F300 ## $a 7 in 9 volumes : $b illustrations (some color, some folded), maps ; $c 30 cm + $e 11 maps (chiefly color, 26 x 18 cm folded) in pockets F336 ## $a text $b txt $2 rdacontent F336 ## $a cartographic image $b cri $2 rdacontent F337 ## $a unmediated $b n $2 rdamedia F338 ## $a volume $b nc $2 rdacarrier F338 ## $a sheet $b nb $2 rdacarrier F500 ## $a Added title page: Twenty-first annual report of the director of the United States Geological Survey. F500 ## $a Parts 1 and 2 have 3 maps in back pockets, parts 3 and 4 have 1 map in back pockets. Part 5 (continued) is a slipcase with 39 maps, consisting mostly of quadrangles showing classification of lands, Part 7 has 7 maps. F500 ## $a "Washington, Government Printing Office, 1900" -- bottom of title page, Parts I, II,and V ; "Washington, Government Printing Office, 1901" -- bottom of title page, Parts III,IV,VII, VII continued, and VII. F500 ## $a Title from title page F504 ## $a Includes bibliographical references and index. F505 2# $a Part 1. Director's report, including triangulation, primary traverse, and spirit leveling. -- Part 2. General geology, economic geology, Alaska: Geology of the Rico Mountains, Colorado / W. Cross and A.C. Spencer. Glacial sculpture of the Bighorn Mountains, Wyoming / F.E. Matthes. The Esmeralda formation, a fresh-water lake deposit; accompanied by a report on the fossil plants of the formation and by a report on a fossil fish / H.W. Turner, F.H. Knowlton and F.A. Lucas. Mineral vein formation at Boulder Hot Springs, Montana / W.H. Weed. Geology of the eastern Choctaw coal field, Indian Territory / J.A. Taff and G.I. Adams. Preliminary report on the Camden coal field of southwestern Arkansas / Taff, J. A. A reconnaissance from Pyramid Harbor to Eagle City, Alaska, including a description of the copper deposits of the Upper White and Tanana Rivers / A. H. Brooks.Preliminary report on the Camden River and the Skolai Mountains, Alaska / O. Rohn -- Preliminary report on a reconnaissance along the Chandlar and Koyukuk rivers, Alaska, in 1899 / F.C. Schrader. Alaskan geographic names / M. Baker. -- Part 3. General geology, ore and phosphate deposits, Philippines: The Newark system of the Pomperaug Valley, Connecticut; with a report on fossil wood from the Newark formation of South Britain, Connecticut / W.H. Hobbs and F.H. Knowlton. The laccoliths of the Black Hills, with a chapter on experiments illustrating intrusion and erosion / T.A. Jaggar, Jr. and E. Howe. The Arkansas bauxite deposits / C.W. Hayes. Tennessee white phosphate / C.W. Hayes. Report on the geology of the Philippine Islands; followed by a version of Ueber Tertiäre fossilien von den Philippinen / G.F. Becker and K. Martin. -- Part 4. Hydrography: Report on the progress of stream measurements for the calendar year 1899 / F.H. Newell. Preliminary description of the geology and water resources of the southern half of the Black Hills and adjoining regions in South Dakota and Wyoming / N.H. Darton. The high plains and their utilization / W.D. Johnson. -- Part 5. Forest reserves: Summary of forestry work in 1899-1900 / Henry Gannett. Lewis and Clarke [sic] forest reserve, Montana / H.B. Ayers. Olympic forest reserve, Washington / Arthur Dodwell, from notes by Theodore F. Rixon. Cascade Range forest reserve from township 28 south to township 37 south, inclusive, together with the Ashland forest reserve and forest regions from township 28 south to township 14 south, inclusive, and from range 2 west to range 14 east, Willamette meridian, inclusive / John B. Leiberg. Stanislaus and Lake Tahoe forest reserves and adjacent territory / George B. Sudworth. Classification of lands / Henry Gannett [and others]. Woodland of Indian Territory / C.H. Fitch. Timber conditions of the pine region of Minnesota / H.B. Ayres. -- Part 5 (continued). Forest reserves maps. -- Part 6. Mineral resources of the United States, 1899 metallic products, coal and coke. -- Part 6 (continued). Mineral resources of the United States, 1899 nonmetallic products, except coal and coke. -- Part 7. Texas: Geography and geology of the Black and Grand Prairies, Texas with detailed descriptions of the cretaceous formations and special reference to artesian waters / by Robert T. Hill. F505 2# $a Part I, Plate I, "Map showing condition and progress of topographic surveys and location of gaging stations for the fiscal year 1899-1900"; Plate II, "Map showing condition and progress of astronomic location, primary triangulation, primary traverse, and spirit leveling for the fiscal year 1899-1900" ; Plate III, "Progress map of topographic and geologic surveys" -- Part III, Plate XXII, "Geologic map of the Rico Mountains, Colorado" ; Plate XXXVII, "Geological map of part of the eastern Choctaw Coal Field" ; Plate XLIII, "Explorations in Alaska 1899 route from Lynn Canal via headwaters of White and Tanana Rivers to Eagle City" -- Part III, Plate LI, "Geological map of a part of the Mesabi District, Minnesota" Part IV, Plate LXV, "Geologic map of the southern half of the Black Hills region in South Dakota and Wyoming" -- Part VII, Plate LXV, "Map of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas including the Eastern and Western Cross Timbers" ; Plate LXVI, "Geology of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas including the Eastern and Western Cross Timbers" ; Plate LXVII, "Cross sections on the lines A-A. B-B etc. PL. LXVI showing geological structure of the Black and Grand Prairies" ; Plate LXVIII, "Location and depths of the artesian wells of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas" ; Plate LXIX, "Map of the Trinity Reservoirs of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas including the Eastern and Western Cross Timbers" ; Plate LXX, "Map of the Paluxy artesian reservoirs of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas including the Eastern and Western Cross Timbers" ; Plate XI, "Map of the Woodbine artesian reservoirs of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas including the Eastern and Western Cross Timbers." F505 2# $a Part V Maps, Plate I, "Forest reserves and national parks" ; Plate III, "Lewis and Clarke Forest Reserve, showing classification of lands" ; Plate XXXIII, "Mount Rainier Forest Reserve, showing classification of lands" ; Plate LI, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing classification of lands" ; Plate LII, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing distribution of red fir" ; Plate LVI, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing distribution of hemlock" ; Plate LIX, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing distribution of cedar" ; Plate LXI, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing distribution of spruce" ; Plate LXIII, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing distribution of silver fir" ; Plate LXXI, "Ashland quadrangle, Oregon, showing classification of lands" ; Plate LXXII, "Klamath quadrangle, Oregon showing classification of lands" ; Plate LXXXV, "Placerville quadrangle, California, showing classification of lands" ; Plate LXXXVI, "Pyramid Peak quadrangle, California, showing classification of lands" ; Plate LXXXVII, "Jackson quadrangle, California, showing classification of lands" ; Plate LXXXVIII, "Big Trees quadrangle, California, showing classification of lands" ; Plate LXXXIX, Markleeville quadrangle, California-Nevada, showing classification of lands" ; Plate XC, "Dardanelles quadrangle, California, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXV, "Sonora quadrangle, California, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXVI, "Yosemite quadrangle, California, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXVII, "Mount Lyell quadrangle, California, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXV, "San Jacinto quadrangle, California, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXVI, "Port Orford quadrangle, California, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXVII, "Coos Bay quadrangle, Oregon, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXVIII, "Roseburg quadrangle, Oregon, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXIX, "Tacoma quadrangle, Washington, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXX, "Seattle quadrangle, Washington, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXXI, "Mount Stuart quadrangle, Washington, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXXII, "Ellensburg quadrangle, Washington, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXXIII, "Chelan quadrangle, Washington, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXXIV, "Spokane quadrangle, Washington, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXXV, "Sandpoint quadrangle, Idaho, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXXVI, "Hamilton quadrangle, Montana-Idaho, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXXVII, "Fortymile quadrangle, Alaska, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXXVIII, "Dayton quadrangle, Wyoming, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXXXIX, "Bald Mountain quadrangle, Wyoming, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXL, "Cloud Peak quadrangle, Wyoming, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXLL, "Newcastle quadrangle, Wyoming-South Dakota, showing classification of lands" ; Plate CXLII, "Indian Territory, showing extent and distribution of woodland" ; Plate CXLIII, "Pine region of Minnesota, showing classification of lands." F590 ## $a UW Libraries has part 5. From the collection of Edward W. Allen. $9 local F650 #0 $a Geology $x Research $z United States. F650 #0 $a Geology $z Alaska. F650 #0 $a Geology $z Philippines. F650 #6 $a Puits artésiens $z Texas. F650 #7 $a Artesian wells $2 fast F650 #7 $a Forest reserves $2 fast F650 #7 $a Geology $2 fast F650 #7 $a Geology $x Research $2 fast F650 #7 $a Mines and mineral resources $2 fast F650 #7 $a Nonmetallic minerals industry $2 fast F650 #7 $a Stream measurements $2 fast F651 #7 $a Alaska $2 fast $1 https://id.oclc.org/worldcat/entity/E39QbtfRq9rTfb3H89Vvrr9x9c F651 #7 $a Philippines $2 fast $1 https://id.oclc.org/worldcat/entity/E39PBJgMFhdtFKxYyQvHvCFkDq F651 #7 $a Texas $2 fast $1 https://id.oclc.org/worldcat/entity/E39PBJj8XjVR9hP7dPxwVtRqcP F651 #7 $a United States $2 fast $1 https://id.oclc.org/worldcat/entity/E39PBJtxgQXMWqmjMjjwXRHgrq F610 20 $a Geological Survey (U.S.) F610 27 $a Geological Survey (U.S.) $2 fast F710 2# $a Geological Survey (U.S.), $e issuing body. $t Annual report. F758 ## $i has work: $a Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 (Text) $1 https://id.oclc.org/worldcat/entity/E39PCFRtv6hXdd3kCHxBbrWGgX $4 https://id.oclc.org/worldcat/ontology/hasWork F700 1# $a Walcott, Charles D. $q (Charles Doolittle), $d 1850-1927, $e author. $1 https://id.oclc.org/worldcat/entity/E39PBJhH6WVrhm78RWh9YjD6rq F700 12 $i Container of (work): $a Hill, Robert Thomas, $d 1858-1941. $t Geography and geology of the Black and Grand Prairies, Texas with detailed descriptions of the cretaceous formations and special reference to artesian waters. rdf:Description.rdawd:P10328: Walcott, Charles D. (Charles Doolittle), 1850-1927, Cross, Whitman, 1854-1949, Spencer, Arthur C. (Arthur Coe), 1871-1964, Matthes, François, 1874-1948, Turner, H. W. (Henry Ward), 1857- Weed, Walter Harvey, 1862-1944, Taff, Joseph A. (Joseph Alexander), 1862-1944, Adams, George I. (George Irving), 1870-1932, Brooks, Alfred H. (Alfred Hulse), 1871-1924, Rohn, Oscar, Schrader, Frank C. (Frank Charles), 1860-1944, Baker, Marcus, 1849-1903, Hobbs, William Herbert, 1864-1952, Jaggar, Thomas Augustus, 1871-1953, Howe, Ernest, 1875-1932, Hayes, C. W. (Charles Willard), 1859-1916, Becker, George F. (George Ferdinand), 1847-1919, Martin, Karl, 1851-1942, Newell, Frederick Haynes, 1862-1932, Darton, Nelson Horatio, 1865-1948, Johnson, Willard D., Gannett, Henry, 1846-1914, Ayres, H. B. (Horace Beemer), 1856- Rixon, Theodore F., Leiberg, John B. (John Bernhard), 1853-1913, Sudworth, George B. (George Bishop), 1864-1927, Fitch, C. H. (Charles Hall), 1854-1930. Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900

rdf:Description.rdaed:P20310: Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 (Cartographic image : Text : English)
rdf:Description.rdaed:P20071: Language of text/sound track or separate title: http://id.loc.gov/vocabulary/languages/eng
rdf:Description.rdaed:P20312: Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900

rdf:Description.rdamd:P30276: Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900 (United States Government Printing Office : Sheet : Volume)
rdf:Description.rdamd:P30156: Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900
rdf:Description.rdamd:P30128: 21st annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900.
rdf:Description.rdamd:P30137: Note on variant title: Twenty-first annual report of the director of the United States Geological Survey
rdf:Description.rdamd:P30128: Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900, Part IDirector's report, including triangulation, primary traverse, and spirit leveling.
Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900, Part IIGeneral geology, economic geology, Alaska.
Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900, Part IIIGeneral geology, ore and phosphate deposits Philippines.
Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900, Part IVHydrography.
Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900, Part VForest Reserves.
Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900, Part V (maps)Forest Reserves.
Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900, Part VIMineral resources of the United States, 1899, metallic products, coal and coke.

Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900, Part VI (continued)Mineral resources of the United States, 1899, Nonmetallic products, except coal and coke.
Twenty-first annual report of the United States Geological Survey to the Secretary of the Interior 1899-1900, Part VIITexas.
rdf:Description.rdamd:P30111: Washington D.C. : United States Government Printing Office, 1900-1901.
rdf:Description.rdamd:P30182: 7 in 9 volumes : illustrations (some color, some folded), maps ; 30 cm + 11 maps (chiefly color, 26 x 18 cm folded) in pockets
rdf:Description.rdamd:P30137: Added title page: Twenty-first annual report of the director of the United States Geological Survey.

Parts 1 and 2 have 3 maps in back pockets, parts 3 and 4 have 1 map in back pockets. Part 5 (continued) is a slipcase with 39 maps, consisting mostly of quadrangles showing classification of lands, Part 7 has 7 maps.

"Washington, Government Printing Office, 1900" -- bottom of title page, Parts I, II,and V ; "Washington, Government Printing Office, 1901" -- bottom of title page, Parts III,IV,VII, VII continued, and VII.

Partial contents: Part 1. Director's report, including triangulation, primary traverse, and spirit leveling. -- Part 2. General geology, economic geology, Alaska: Geology of the Rico Mountains, Colorado / W. Cross and A.C. Spencer. Glacial sculpture of the Bighorn Mountains, Wyoming / F.E. Matthes. The Esmeralda formation, a fresh-water lake deposit; accompanied by a report on the fossil plants of the formation and by a report on a fossil fish / H.W. Turner, F.H. Knowlton and F.A. Lucas. Mineral vein formation at Boulder Hot Springs, Montana / W.H. Weed. Geology of the eastern Choctaw coal field, Indian Territory / J.A. Taff and G.I. Adams. Preliminary report on the Camden coal field of southwestern Arkansas / Taff, J. A. A reconnaissance from Pyramid Harbor to Eagle City, Alaska, including a description of the copper deposits of the Upper White and Tanana Rivers / A. H. Brooks.Preliminary report on the Camden River and the Skolai Mountains, Alaska / O. Rohn -- Preliminary report on a reconnaissance along the Chandlar and Koyukuk rivers, Alaska, in 1899 / F.C. Schrader. Alaskan geographic names / M. Baker. -- Part 3. General geology, ore and phosphate deposits, Philippines: The Newark system of the Pomperaug Valley, Connecticut; with a report on fossil wood from the Newark formation of South Britain, Connecticut / W.H. Hobbs and F.H. Knowlton. The laccoliths of the Black Hills, with a chapter on experiments illustrating intrusion and erosion / T.A. Jaggar, Jr. and E. Howe. The Arkansas bauxite deposits / C.W. Hayes. Tennessee white phosphate / C.W. Hayes. Report on the geology of the Philippine Islands; followed by a version of Ueber Tertiäre fossilien von den Philippinen / G.F. Becker and K. Martin. -- Part 4. Hydrography: Report on the progress of stream measurements for the calendar year 1899 / F.H. Newell. Preliminary description of the geology and water resources of the southern half of the Black Hills and adjoining regions in South Dakota and Wyoming / N.H. Darton. The high plains and their utilization / W.D. Johnson. -- Part 5. Forest reserves: Summary of forestry work in 1899-1900 / Henry Gannett. Lewis and Clarke [sic] forest reserve, Montana / H.B. Ayers. Olympic forest reserve, Washington / Arthur Dodwell, from notes by Theodore F. Rixon. Cascade Range forest reserve from township 28 south to township 37 south, inclusive, together with the Ashland forest reserve and forest regions from township 28 south to township 14 south, inclusive, and from range 2 west to range 14 east, Willamette meridian, inclusive / John B. Leiberg. Stanislaus and Lake Tahoe forest reserves and adjacent territory / George B. Sudworth. Classification of lands / Henry Gannett [and others]. Woodland of Indian Territory / C.H. Fitch. Timber conditions of the pine region of Minnesota / H.B. Ayres. -- Part 5 (continued). Forest reserves maps. -- Part 6. Mineral resources of the United States, 1899 metallic products, coal and coke. -- Part 6 (continued). Mineral resources of the United States, 1899 nonmetallic products, except coal and coke. -- Part 7. Texas: Geography and geology of the Black and Grand Prairies, Texas with detailed descriptions of the cretaceous formations and special reference to artesian waters / by Robert T. Hill.

Partial contents: Part I, Plate I, "Map showing condition and progress of topographic surveys and location of gaging stations for the fiscal year 1899-1900"; Plate II, "Map showing condition and progress of astronomic location, primary triangulation, primary traverse, and spirit leveling for the fiscal year 1899-1900" ; Plate III, "Progress map of topographic and geologic surveys" -- Part III, Plate XXII, "Geologic map of the Rico Mountains, Colorado" ; Plate XXXVII, "Geological map of part of the eastern Choctaw Coal Field" ; Plate XLIII, "Explorations in Alaska 1899 route from Lynn Canal via headwaters of White and Tanana Rivers to Eagle City" -- Part III, Plate LI, "Geological map of a part of the Mesabi District, Minnesota" Part IV, Plate LXV, "Geologic map of the southern half of the Black Hills region in South Dakota and Wyoming" -- Part VII, Plate LXV, "Map of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas including the Eastern and Western Cross Timbers" ; Plate LXVI, "Geology of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas including the Eastern and Western Cross Timbers" ; Plate LXVII, "Cross sections on the lines A-A. B-B etc. PL. LXVI showing geological structure of the Black and Grand Prairies" ; Plate LXVIII, "Location and depths of the artesian wells of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas" ; Plate LXIX, "Map of the Trinity Reservoirs of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas including the Eastern and Western Cross Timbers" ; Plate LXX, "Map of the Paluxy artesian reservoirs of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas including the Eastern and Western Cross Timbers" ; Plate XI, "Map of the Woodbine artesian reservoirs of the Black and Grand Prairies of Texas including the Eastern and Western Cross Timbers."

Partial contents: Part V Maps, Plate I, "Forest reserves and national parks" ; Plate III, "Lewis and Clarke Forest Reserve, showing classification of lands" ; Plate XXXIII, "Mount Rainier Forest Reserve, showing classification of lands" ; Plate LI, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing classification of lands" ; Plate LII, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing distribution of red fir" ; Plate LVI, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing distribution of hemlock" ; Plate LIX, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing distribution of cedar" ; Plate LXI, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing distribution of spruce" ; Plate LXIII, "Olympic Forest Reserve, showing distribution of silver fir" ;
